title: শান্তির জন্য জাতিসংঘের উদ্যোগে বাংলাদেশ প্রতিশ্রুতিবদ্ধ: পররাষ্ট্র উপদেষ্টা
comment: This report on a UN Security Council speech mentions Rohingya refugees factually, noting Bangladesh has sheltered over 1.2 million fleeing atrocities and that 20,000 more recently crossed the border, exceeding capacity. The framing presents Rohingya as victims of violence in Myanmar and casts Bangladesh's hosting role sympathetically, but this is conveyed through the official's quoted statement in a diplomatic context rather than the reporter's own commentary on the Rohingya identity itself.

title: আশ্রয়শিবিরে রোহিঙ্গাদের জন্য স্থায়ী অবকাঠামো নয়
comment: This report on an NGO press conference frames Rohingya presence primarily through the lens of environmental damage (deforestation, landslides, water scarcity) their camps have caused, and argues permanent infrastructure would make Rohingya refuse repatriation. While not explicitly hostile, the framing centers Rohingya as an environmental and policy burden rather than as people with agency, giving a mildly negative cast via the emphasis on problems caused by their presence, though this stems from quoted NGO positions rather than the reporter's editorializing.

title: টেকনাফে অস্ত্রের মুখে ঘর থেকে ব্যবসায়ীকে অপহরণ, এপিবিএনের সঙ্গে গোলাগুলি
comment: This crime report explicitly identifies the armed kidnappers as Rohingya nationals and repeatedly references 'Rohingya militants/criminals' (রোহিঙ্গা সন্ত্রাসী) demanding extortion from local businesses, attributing the abduction and violence directly to this group. The consistent framing of Rohingya as armed criminals/extortionists engaged in violent crime against a local Bangladeshi victim constitutes an unfavorable, threat-associated portrayal.

title: রোহিঙ্গা শিবিরে বন্ধ শিক্ষাকেন্দ্র চালু হচ্ধে, ডিএমএইচ ফাউন্ডেশনের উদ্যোগ
comment: This report on an NGO's education and healthcare initiative for Rohingya includes strongly sympathetic quotes describing them as 'the most persecuted people in the world' living inhumanely, framing their plight in terms of justice, accountability, and moral obligation. The overall tone, while largely relaying activists' quotes, is compassionate and humanizing toward Rohingya as vulnerable refugees deserving support.

title: উখিয়ায় রোহিঙ্গা আশ্রয়শিবিরে ঘরে ঢুকে সন্ত্রাসীদের গুলি, বাবা ও দুই সন্তান নিহত
comment: This crime report details an armed-group killing within a Rohingya camp, explicitly naming Rohingya militant factions (ARSA, RSO) as perpetrators and victims, and citing statistics of dozens of Rohingya killed in inter-factional violence over drug trafficking turf. While factual and sourced to police/camp leaders, the cumulative effect of detailing repeated Rohingya-on-Rohingya militant violence, camp terror, and criminal networks casts the community (via its armed factions) in a negative, threat-associated light, though ordinary residents are also depicted as victims living in fear.

title: উখিয়া সীমান্তের ওপারে গোলাগুলি, রোহিঙ্গা যুবক গুলিবিদ্ধ
comment: This report describes cross-border fighting in Myanmar between Rohingya armed groups (ARSA, RSO) and the Arakan Army, with a Rohingya youth wounded and local Bangladeshi residents living in fear from stray gunfire. The framing is factual/geopolitical, treating Rohingya armed factions as one party in a broader regional conflict rather than singling out ordinary Rohingya for blame, keeping the tone largely neutral-informational.

title: রোহিঙ্গা শিবিরে অস্ত্রধারীদের বিরুদ্ধে ব্যবস্থা নেওয়া হবে: স্বরাষ্ট্র উপদেষ্টা
comment: This report on a government camp visit discusses plans to act against 'armed and terrorist groups' in Rohingya camps, but also includes an advisor's sympathetic framing that Bangladesh has given humane shelter and that the international community bears responsibility for the Rohingya crisis. The article balances security concerns about armed elements with humanitarian framing of the broader refugee population, resulting in a largely neutral, policy-focused tone.

title: ইউএসএআইডির তহবিল: বাংলাদেশের উন্নয়নের গতিপথ কি হুমকির মুখে
comment: This opinion piece on USAID funding cuts mentions the Rohingya crisis only briefly as one of several sectors affected by reduced foreign aid, without any characterization of Rohingya people themselves. The identity is essentially incidental to the article's main focus on development funding.

title: মিয়ানমার নিয়ে আমাদের কূটনৈতিক সক্ষমতা বাড়াতে হবে
comment: This geopolitical interview discusses the Rohingya crisis primarily as a diplomatic and security challenge for Bangladesh, referencing repatriation failures and regional power dynamics. Rohingya people are framed as a policy/security issue subject of diplomacy rather than as individuals characterized positively or negatively; the tone toward the identity itself remains analytical and neutral.

title: কক্সবাজারে রোহিঙ্গা শিবিরে শিক্ষক ছাঁটাইয়ের প্রতিবাদে বিক্ষোভ, সড়ক অবরোধ
comment: This report on a protest by dismissed Bangladeshi teachers includes their allegation that Rohingya and outside teachers were unfairly retained while local teachers were fired, calling it discriminatory ('বৈষম্য'). This frames Rohingya (as beneficiaries of retained jobs) in a mildly negative light by association with perceived unfair preferential treatment, though this is a quoted grievance from protesting teachers rather than the reporter's own framing.

title: রোহিঙ্গা সমস্যায় রাজনৈতিক সমাধান খুঁজে বের করতে হবে
comment: This report on a policy dialogue frames Rohingya as victims of atrocities and genocide deserving justice, safe repatriation, protected citizenship, and education, with speakers explicitly calling for accountability against perpetrators. The overall tone is sympathetic and rights-oriented toward Rohingya, conveyed through quoted experts and advocates in a policy-focused, dignified register.

title: আসিয়ান জোট রোহিঙ্গা সংকটের মূল কারণকে উপেক্ষা করেছে: এপিএইচআর
comment: This report on an ASEAN human rights body's press conference frames Rohingya sympathetically as a vulnerable population facing looming famine, inadequate humanitarian funding, and need for education/repatriation, calling for urgent, compassionate international action. The overall tone is advocacy-oriented and sympathetic toward the Rohingya plight, conveyed through quoted officials.
